questions: The brief says 'secondary clips maintain their relative track offsets from the anchor.' If clips span 3 source tracks (V1, V2, V3) and the anchor is on V2, dragging to create a new track at top means: anchor → new track, V1 clip → ???(above new track = out of bounds), V3 clip → old V1. Should we reject multi-drag to new track when some secondary clips would need tracks above/below the new track that don't exist? Or should we create multiple new tracks?

Should the drop indicator during multi-drag show the 'Drop to create new track' label (currently only shown for single-clip drags since DropIndicator.tsx:106 hides the ghost/line when isNewTrack)? Or should it show the ghost overlay at the edge?
Is rowsToConfig exported and usable for converting applyMultiDragMoves output back to a config? If not, should we go with the config-level clip update approach in Step 2 instead?

success_criteria.criterion: Multi-clip drag to the new-track zone (above top row / below bottom row) creates a new track and moves all selected clips with correct relative offsets
Single-clip drag to new track continues to work identically (code path untouched)
Multi-clip drag to existing tracks continues to work identically
planMultiDragMoves returns canMove: false when any secondary clip would land out of bounds after the new track is added
Track kind compatibility is enforced — cannot create a visual track and place audio clips on it
All existing video-editor tests pass without modification (except tests that explicitly test the old skipping behavior)
New unit tests cover: new track at top, new track at bottom, multi-track selection, kind mismatch rejection
Secondary ghost indicators render correctly when dragging multi-clip selection near timeline edges
Drop indicator shows new-track affordance during multi-clip drag near edges
New functions are pure (no side effects) and follow existing patterns in multi-drag-utils.ts
No new files created beyond the test file for multi-drag-utils (if it doesn't exist)

assumptions: Rejecting multi-drag when any secondary clip would land out of bounds (even after adding the new track) is acceptable — this matches current planMultiDragMoves behavior. We won't create multiple new tracks.
The anchor clip's source track kind determines the new track's kind. All clips in the selection that would land on the new track must be the same kind.
The type: 'config' edit path is the right choice for the new-track multi-drag case, since it naturally handles both track addition and clip movement in one atomic edit.

Ghost rendering during drag (computeSecondaryGhosts) needs to handle the virtual new-track row to avoid ghosts disappearing at edges, but this is a polish item — the core functionality (drop creating the track) takes priority.
We assume the existing DropIndicator new-track visual (the CSS class drop-target-new-track) is sufficient for multi-drag — we don't need a separate multi-drag new-track indicator.
